nodes.name: Loop Over Items
nodes.notes:
- Loop through the data received from Google Sheets. I set the batch size to 10 because the API limit allows a maximum of 10 requests per second. If we exceed this limit, we will receive a 429 status code.

- Do not rename this node. If you do rename it, make sure to update line 2 in the JavaScript node: const originalItem = $('Loop Over Items').item.json;

nodes.name: Get CSV data
nodes.notes: - Read the CSV data and make sure it contains an updated_similarweb_api_data column.

nodes.name: Javascript
nodes.notes:
- Get the original data from previous node its like "remember which row and domain triggered this api request"

- Get the response from the http request

- Return data such as row_number, gamer_id, approved_domain, updated_similarweb_api_data, processing_status, last_updated

- If there's a data it will return raw data, if no data it return 0 - no visits data available, if there's an error it will return 0 - status_code

nodes.name: Get: SimilarWeb Data
nodes.notes:
- We use the Similarweb API data and send parameters with the request. Since Similarweb is protected by Cloudflare, it does not allow bots to scrape its data.

- We also use the approved_domain column as a parameter when calling the Similarweb endpoint.

- Randomize user-agent

nodes.name: Update CSV data
nodes.notes:
- I used row_number for match on since it’s the only unique field.

- update the updated_similarweb_api_data column based on what we get from http request

parameters.content:
## SimilarWeb

**Purpose:**
- This workflow automation removes the need for manual checking of domain visit data in Similarweb. The process automatically retrieves the approved_domain from a CSV file, uses it to search data in Similarweb, and extracts the visit metrics such as Visits or EstimatedMonthlyVisits. The retrieved data is then used to update the CSV file, ensuring accurate and efficient domain traffic tracking without manual input.


**Developer:** Justin Peligro 
**Date:** January 2026

## Documentation
- This workflow will only trigger when the user types Run in the input field of the form.

- Import the data in google sheet and add a column updated_similarweb_api_data

-

parameters.formDescription: Type “Run” to start the workflow. Wait for the workflow to finish, it will automatically populate the updated_similarweb_api_data column in your sheet.

nodes.name: Trigger
nodes.notes: - This workflow will trigger when user type 'Run'

parameters.content:
## Logic Node
- Check if the user type "Run" if yes get the data in sheet, if no, stop the workflow.

nodes.name: Edit Fields
nodes.notes:
- Implement a regex for approved_domain column to avoid bad api request.

**Regex Requirements:**
- Remove subdomains
- Remove the trailing slash "/" from approved_domain
- Keep http at the beginning

nodes.name: RANDOMIZE WAIT
nodes.notes: - Wait for 5-15 seconds before we process another batch of data
